perturbation.original_text: "Customer Gas" shall mean Gas delivered by or on behalf of Customer for transportation on the Transportation System pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: "Customer Gas" shall mean Gas delivered by or on behalf of Customer for transportation on the Transportation System pursuant to this Agreement, excluding any Gas that does not meet Transporter's quality specifications.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Customer Gas' is broad, encompassing all gas delivered by the customer. By adding 'excluding any Gas that does not meet Transporter's quality specifications,' a contradiction is introduced. Article VII states that 'Customer Gas received at a Point of Receipt from one of the Plants shall be deemed to be in compliance with the applicable Gas quality specifications of the Transportation System'. Therefore, the modification causes an in-text contradiction because the new definition implies that gas from the plants could be excluded, but Article VII asserts it is always deemed compliant. Now, it is uncertain whether gas from the plants is actually 'Customer Gas' if Transporter says it doesn't meet gas quality specifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, Customer Gas received at a Point of Receipt from one of the Plants shall be deemed to be in compliance with the applicable Gas quality specifications of the Transportation System.
perturbation.justification: The change to the definition of 'Customer Gas' introduces a contradiction with the clause stating that 'Customer Gas received at a Point of Receipt from one of the Plants shall be deemed to be in compliance with the applicable Gas quality specifications'. The modified definition excludes gas that does not meet Transporter's quality specifications, implying that gas from the plants could be excluded. However, the contradicted text asserts that such gas is always deemed compliant, which causes a direct conflict and uncertainty regarding whether the gas from plants is 'Customer Gas' if Transporter deems it of bad quality. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, where conflicting implications are introduced.

perturbation.original_text: "Transportation System" shall mean the Commencement Date Facilities and all other equipment, devices, Measurement Facilities, pipelines and other facilities owned or operated by Transporter at or downstream of the Points of Receipt, necessary to receive Customer Gas at the Points of Receipt for transport to the Points of Delivery, and all additions, modifications, alterations, replacements, extensions, or expansions made thereto by Transporter from time to time, including, without limitation, any New Facilities pursuant to Article II hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: "Transportation System" shall mean the Commencement Date Facilities and all other equipment, devices, Measurement Facilities, pipelines and other facilities owned or operated by Transporter at or downstream of the Points of Receipt, necessary to receive Customer Gas at the Points of Receipt for transport to the Points of Delivery, but does not include any New Facilities built after the Commencement Date, regardless of Article II.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Transportation System' explicitly includes 'any New Facilities pursuant to Article II hereof'. The modified version directly contradicts this by stating that it 'does not include any New Facilities built after the Commencement Date, regardless of Article II.' This creates a significant in-text contradiction and uncertainty about the scope of the 'Transportation System' because Article II discusses how New Facilities could be built after the Commencement Date. Now it is uncertain whether any New Facilities are part of the Transportation system as defined in the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Post-Commencement Date Facilities. If after the Commencement Date, Customer requires an addition, modification, alteration, replacement, or expansion of the Transportation System (a "New Facility"), Customer will provide Transporter a written notification of such requested New Facility containing sufficient information for Transporter to estimate the cost to provide such New Facility. Although Transporter shall have no obligation to provide a requested New Facility, Transporter may, in its sole discretion, agree to construct or acquire such New Facility. The commercial terms applicable the construction or acquisition of any New Facility will be determined in accordance with the AMI/MEA Agreement. The Parties agree to make any revisions to this Agreement (including the Exhibits hereto) that are necessary to reflect any New Facility.
perturbation.justification: The modified text explicitly excludes 'any New Facilities built after the Commencement Date' from the definition of 'Transportation System', directly contradicting the 'Contradicted Text' which discusses the process for adding 'New Facilities' to the 'Transportation System' post-commencement. The original definition explicitly included New Facilities pursuant to Article II. This change introduces a significant in-text contradiction regarding whether New Facilities are considered part of the Transportation System, causing uncertainty and potential disputes.

perturbation.original_text: Term and Termination. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue in full force and effect until the end of the fifteenth (15th) Contract Year, and shall continue in full force and effect thereafter until terminated by either Party by providing thirty (30) calendar days' prior written notice of termination to the other Party (such fifteen (15) Contract Year period, as may be further extended as provided herein is referred to as the "Term").
perturbation.changed_text: Term and Termination. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue in full force and effect until the end of the fifteenth (15th) Contract Year, unless terminated earlier by Transporter due to Customer's failure to meet gas quality standards at any point. The agreement continues thereafter until terminated by either Party providing thirty (30) calendar days' prior written notice of termination to the other Party (such fifteen (15) Contract Year period, as may be further extended as provided herein is referred to as the "Term").
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a clear term of fifteen years, subject to extension and termination by either party with 30 days' notice. The added clause allows Transporter to terminate the agreement *earlier* if Customer fails to meet gas quality standards. However, Article VII states that Customer Gas received from the plants is *deemed* to be in compliance with gas quality specifications. This creates a contradiction: Transporter can terminate for gas quality failures, but gas from the plants (the primary source according to other clauses) is always considered compliant. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the actual term of the agreement and the circumstances under which it can be terminated, especially when considering gas from the Plants.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, Customer Gas received at a Point of Receipt from one of the Plants shall be deemed to be in compliance with the applicable Gas quality specifications of the Transportation System.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition where the Transporter can terminate the agreement earlier than the originally specified 15-year term due to Customer's failure to meet gas quality standards. However, the contradicted text states that Customer Gas received from the Plants is *deemed* to be in compliance with gas quality specifications. This creates a direct contradiction: the Transporter *can* terminate for gas quality failures, but gas from the Plants (the primary source according to other clauses) is *always* considered compliant. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction, as it creates uncertainty about the actual term of the agreement and the circumstances under which it can be terminated, particularly concerning gas from the Plants.
